title: 成语 from GH
description: Classic Chinese idioms (chengyu) — each a four-character story with a moral.

items.english: to play the lute to a cow — to talk over someone's head; preach to deaf ears
items.example_sentence: 跟他解释这么深的道理，简直是对牛弹琴。

items.english: the fox borrows the tiger's might — to bully others by flaunting powerful connections
items.example_sentence: 他不过是狐假虎威，仗着老板的势力欺负人。

items.english: to wait by a tree stump for a rabbit — to count on lucky windfalls instead of working
items.example_sentence: 不努力工作，光想守株待兔是不行的。

items.english: to paint a dragon and dot in the eyes — to add the finishing touch that brings something to life
items.example_sentence: 他最后一句话真是画龙点睛。

items.english: to mend the pen after the sheep are lost — better late than never
items.example_sentence: 虽然出了问题，亡羊补牢还来得及。

items.english: to look at flowers from a galloping horse — a hurried, superficial glance
items.example_sentence: 这次旅行时间太短，只能走马看花。

items.english: to have a finished bamboo in one's chest — to have a well-thought-out plan in mind
items.example_sentence: 上台演讲前，他已经胸有成竹。

items.english: to quench one's thirst by gazing at plums — to console oneself with illusions
items.example_sentence: 光做白日梦只是望梅止渴。

items.english: to swallow a date whole — to read or learn without understanding
items.example_sentence: 学习不能囫囵吞枣，要慢慢消化。

items.english: a peach-blossom land beyond this world — a utopia, a hidden paradise
items.example_sentence: 这个小村庄简直是世外桃源。

items.english: lucky in three lifetimes — a great honor or privilege
items.example_sentence: 能认识您，真是三生有幸。

items.english: three people make a tiger — a rumor repeated enough becomes accepted as truth
items.example_sentence: 三人成虎，谣言听多了人们就信了。

items.english: three in the morning, four at night — fickle, changeable, inconstant
items.example_sentence: 他做事朝三暮四，让人无法信任。

items.english: practice makes perfect — skill comes from familiarity
items.example_sentence: 多练习就好了，熟能生巧。

items.english: to cover one's ears while stealing a bell — to deceive oneself
items.example_sentence: 你这样隐瞒错误，不过是掩耳盗铃。

items.english: so happy one forgets Shu — too content in pleasure to think of home
items.example_sentence: 他在国外玩得乐不思蜀，都不想回来了。

items.english: to mark the boat to find a dropped sword — to cling to old ways despite changed circumstances
items.example_sentence: 时代变了，还用老办法就是刻舟求剑。

items.english: Mao Sui recommends himself — to volunteer for a task or position
items.example_sentence: 他毛遂自荐，担任了项目负责人。

items.english: the old frontiersman loses his horse — a blessing in disguise
items.example_sentence: 塞翁失马，焉知非福。

items.english: to return the jade intact to Zhao — to return a borrowed item safe and sound
items.example_sentence: 你借的书我看完了，一定完璧归赵。

items.english: to weave the roof before it rains — to plan ahead, prepare for trouble
items.example_sentence: 做事要未雨绸缪，不能临时抱佛脚。

items.english: to wield an axe at Lu Ban's gate — to show off in front of an expert
items.example_sentence: 在大师面前谈艺术，真是班门弄斧。

items.english: to pull up seedlings to help them grow — to ruin things by rushing
items.example_sentence: 教孩子不能拔苗助长，要有耐心。

items.english: the man of Qi worries that the sky will fall — to entertain groundless fears
items.example_sentence: 别杞人忧天了，事情没那么糟。

items.english: spear and shield contradicting each other — to contradict oneself
items.example_sentence: 他说的话前后自相矛盾。

items.english: to pad the orchestra with a fake flute player — to pose as a specialist to make up the numbers
items.example_sentence: 他根本不会，是在滥竽充数。

items.english: Yelang thinks itself great — provincial conceit; ignorant arrogance
items.example_sentence: 井底之蛙夜郎自大，眼界太窄。

items.english: the Guizhou donkey has exhausted its tricks — at the end of one's rope
items.example_sentence: 他黔驴技穷，再也想不出办法了。

items.english: to rise at the cock's crow and practice the sword — diligent and self-disciplined
items.example_sentence: 他每天闻鸡起舞，刻苦练功。

items.english: to draw a cake to satisfy hunger — to feed on illusions
items.example_sentence: 光许诺不兑现，等于画饼充饥。

items.english: heard on the road, told on the way — hearsay, gossip, unverified rumor
items.example_sentence: 这些都是道听途说，不可全信。

items.english: to view a leopard through a tube — to see only one part of the picture
items.example_sentence: 只看一面就下结论，是管中窥豹。

items.english: to hide one's illness and avoid the doctor — to conceal faults to one's own harm
items.example_sentence: 有错误就要改，不要讳疾忌医。

items.english: to find a horse by following the picture — to follow clues mechanically without flexibility
items.example_sentence: 做事不能死板地按图索骥。

items.english: to buy the box and return the pearl — to show poor judgment of value
items.example_sentence: 他舍本逐末，简直是买椟还珠。

items.english: to climb a tree to catch a fish — to use a hopelessly wrong method
items.example_sentence: 用错误的方法解决问题，就是缘木求鱼。

items.english: to drain the pond to catch all the fish — short-sighted greed that destroys the source
items.example_sentence: 竭泽而渔的做法不可持续。

items.english: Dongshi imitates the frown — a clumsy imitation that achieves the opposite effect
items.example_sentence: 她照搬别人的风格，结果东施效颦。

items.english: to draw legs on a snake — to ruin something by adding what is unnecessary
items.example_sentence: 原本写得很好，加这段反而画蛇添足。

items.english: to give up halfway — to quit before finishing what you started
items.example_sentence: 做事要坚持到底，不能半途而废。
